meta.rationale: Original BCT-vs-NCT DiD violates SUTVA: 31/35 NCT tokens also appear in BCT; ~14.9% of BCT tonnage comes from wallets that also deposit in NCT. Fisher z-test assumes independence; this script supplies three valid alternatives.

approach_1_non_overlapping_subsets.conclusion: DiD NOT IDENTIFIED in this design. All 4 NCT-only tokens are unscored, leaving zero scored deposits on the control side. The BCT-only side still degrades (rho=-0.413, p=6.52e-35), confirming that degradation is not driven solely by the shared-token subset — but without a control arm there is no DiD. Non-overlapping-subset design is infeasible with current scoring coverage.
warnings: NCT-only subset tiny (n=0); Spearman p unreliable, DiD underpowered.

approach_2_cluster_robust_bootstrap.conclusion: Cluster-robust inference does NOT preserve significance.

approach_3_within_bct_credit_type.rationale: Same pool, same depositor universe, only credit type varies. Eliminates SUTVA contamination by holding pool-design and depositor-composition fixed.
approach_3_within_bct_credit_type.conclusion: Within-BCT DiD does not significantly distinguish renewable from nature.

notes: MCO2 is an ERC-20 on Ethereum/Polygon but it is NOT a permissionless deposit pool like BCT/NCT --- it is a fixed-supply retirement token issued by Moss against specific bridged REDD+ credits. There are no ongoing 'Deposited' events to fetch. Only static composition data is available (n=30 synthetic archetype credits in pool_analyzer.py).

MCO2 therefore cannot serve as a temporal-analysis control. It can still be cited cross-sectionally (Lemons Index = 0.713) as evidence that other open-acceptance tokens suffer similar quality problems, but not as a DiD control arm.
approach_4_mco2_alternative_control.conclusion: MCO2 unavailable as a DiD control: no on-chain deposit stream.

overall_assessment.headline: The original BCT-vs-NCT pool-level DiD is NOT an identified causal estimate because 88.6% of NCT tokens are also in BCT, and once token-level dependence is modelled via cluster-robust bootstrap SEs the rho difference is no longer distinguishable from zero (p=0.24). None of the three valid designs preserves the original DiD at alpha=0.05 under cluster-robust inference. What DOES survive is the descriptive finding that renewable credits degrade sharply within BCT (rho=-0.50, n=707) while nature-based credits do not (rho=-0.10, n=97) — reported honestly with the caveat that cluster-robust SEs do not reject equality of the two rhos.
approaches_that_undermine_claim: cluster-robust pool-level comparison: SEs inflate once token dependence is honoured, and the BCT-NCT rho difference is no longer distinguishable at alpha=0.05.

non-overlapping-subset DiD is UNIDENTIFIED: all 4 NCT-only tokens are unscored, so the control arm has zero scored deposits. Best we can say is that BCT-only (n=814) still shows rho=-0.413, ruling out shared-token churn as sole driver.

within-BCT DiD: naive Fisher z is highly significant (p=3.6e-05) but cluster-robust bootstrap p=0.203 — the SE inflates because many BCT tokens are concentrated in a small number of methodology clusters with temporally clumped deposits.

non_overlapping_subset.conclusion: DiD NOT IDENTIFIED in this design. All 4 NCT-only tokens are unscored, leaving zero scored deposits on the control side. The BCT-only side still degrades (rho=-0.413, p=6.52e-35), confirming that degradation is not driven solely by the shared-token subset — but without a control arm there is no DiD. Non-overlapping-subset design is infeasible with current scoring coverage.

mco2_control.reason: No on-chain deposit stream for MCO2 (fixed-supply token).

overall_assessment.recommended_paper_claim: Drop the BCT-vs-NCT DiD as a causal claim. The paper should report: (i) BCT-only Spearman rho=-0.413 (p=6.5e-35, n=814) as a within-treatment temporal degradation fact — robust to excluding shared tokens; (ii) within-BCT descriptive decomposition rho_renewable=-0.50 vs rho_nature=-0.10 as suggestive evidence of adverse selection by credit type, with the explicit caveat that a cluster-robust bootstrap (token-clustered, n_boot=5000) yields p=0.20 for the rho difference — i.e. we CANNOT reject equality under correct inference. This is a much weaker empirical claim than the original paper's DiD. Retracting the causal DiD and retreating to descriptive correlation is the statistically defensible path.
